<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking id=13 + purple swatch
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current objects:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['id'] in [12, 13, 14]:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Compare with previous frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    prev_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes']}
    curr_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in nodes}
    print("\nDiff after click id=13 + purple swatch:")
    for i in [12, 13, 14]:
        if i in prev_nodes and i in curr_nodes:
            pc = prev_nodes[i]['color']
            cc = curr_nodes[i]['color']
            if pc != cc:
                print(f"  id={i}: {pc} → {cc}")
            else:
                print(f"  id={i}: {pc} (no change)")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
No change at all. So clicking id=13 (purple part of bottom rect) + purple swatch does nothing. Let me think about what I've tried:
1. Click top rect (id=12) + white swatch → top becomes white
2. Click top rect (id=12) + purple swatch → top becomes purple  
3. Click bottom rect purple part (id=13) + white swatch → top becomes white
4. Click bottom rect white part (id=14) + purple swatch → NO CHANGE
5. Click bottom rect purple part (id=13) + purple swatch → NO CHANGE
6. SPACE → NO CHANGE
So the pattern seems to be:
- Clicking id=12 (top rect) + color → changes id=12 to that color
- Clicking id=13 (purple part of bottom rect) + color → changes id=12 to that color (same as clicking top)
- Clicking id=14 (white part of bottom rect) + color → NO CHANGE
So I can control id=12 but not id=14. SPACE doesn't seem to do anything.
Wait, maybe I need to look at this differently. The bottom rectangle has TWO parts - a purple top (id=13) and a white bottom (id=14). Maybe the goal is to make id=14 purple too. But I can't click on id=14 and have any effect.
Let me try clicking on the white part of id=14 with the WHITE swatch instead.
